asset_generation_entry: 1.1 Hero bee animation rig — humanoid bee-person direction
status: LIVE on the Player. Wired via Assets/Editor/BuildTools/HeroBeePersonSkinBuilder.cs into Assets/Sprites/HeroBeePerson/HeroBeePersonGenerated.asset (SpriteLibraryAsset). Confirmed via 92/92 EditMode tests, scene validator PASSED, visual drive screenshots.
supersedes: Assets/Sprites/HeroBee/ (insect-bodied direction, rejected 2026-07-08). Left intact, unused.

generation_params.description: A male humanoid bee-person hero — yellow skin, short black hair, small branching antler-like antennae on his head, wearing a simple shirt and pants, two small translucent insect wings on his back, and a large, prominent bee-abdomen tail extending from his lower body with bold yellow-and-black stripes, tapering to a sharp point. This striped tail is his stinger weapon and must be clearly visible, not a minor detail. Confident heroic stance.
generation_params.note: output canvas 40x40 — rescale to 32x32 Character tier on import per ASSET_GENERATION.md 0.1

direction_split.note: User's explicit choice: south for idle/hit/death, west for run/attack — two different facings from the same character generation, chosen for how well each angle reads that particular motion. RESOLVED in code: user chose 'add code support for 2 facings' over regenerating art. See CharacterAnimator._idleUsesMirroredFacing (set false on the Player only) — Idle stays Fixed to the south pose, Run/Attack still mirror the west pose for movement/aim direction.

attack.mode: v3 custom (no template covers a tail-curl attack)
attack.action_description: a bee-tail stinger attack: the long striped tail curls forward from behind the body, sweeping under and between the legs, then extends and lengthens forward as if unfurling to deliver a strike, finally thrusting to release a projectile from its tip
attack.user_fallback_instruction: if it requires too many frames then remove the expansion part — kept the full curl+expand+release version at 9 frames since it fit within a single v3 call; revisit if it reads as muddled once seen at actual game scale/speed.
attack.in_engine_frame_selection: the shared Attack.anim clip only resolves 6 distinct SpriteResolver labels, so HeroBeePersonSkinBuilder down-samples to indices [0,2,3,5,6,8] (keeps first and last frame exact, evenly spreads the middle) rather than using all 9.

customization_plan.status: documented only, not built — same as before, saving the design for later
slots: antlers color (the yellow dots at the antler tips)
customization_plan.supersedes_note: Replaces the earlier speculative customization list (body/hair/stinger-stripe/shirt/pants/shoes color, hair style) now that there's an actual approved design to base it on — this list is grounded in what's actually visible on the generated character.

generation_cost: This character total: 2 (v3 base) + 4 (template clips: idle/hit/death/run) + 1 (v3 custom attack) = 7 generations.

folder_layout.base/south.png, base/west.png: reference rotation frames (unused directly, idle/run source their own animated frame 0 from these)
folder_layout.attack/0-8.png: west-facing attack frames (frame 0 is the reference pose, 1-8 generated)
